mechanics.raw_text:
Grund- G will never forgive him, Guess who is the designated scapegoat hencefortht 
Withe Space Suits ‘Once the forms are taken care of, Grund-G observes, “These suits have been issued tous, 0 we're going to use them, Any questions? No? Then let's suit up.”” When the crates are opened the sults are covered with dust, and the PCs can find no manuals. Zim-R's response to questions about the suits is "I'm sorry. That information is not available at this time.” The information is not Available because no manual has been discovered and the suits have not been used in the past century. “The sults are ancient, cumbersome hardsuts with air recirculation and powerful heating/ cooling systems, The controls for these systems are located on the chest ofthe sults The suits have a hefty negative buoyancy. (That means they sink real good.) ‘The charge inthe batteries forthe recircula- tion and heating/cooling systems js. quite low alter years of storage, but they will be good {or about one hour of operation if nothing else ‘goes wrong, (Fat chance.) Unfortunately the jauges only indicate the strength ofthe bat. tery charge, not how long the power is good for, so the PCs will have to keep an eye on the gauges and pay close attention to warn- 
ing lights, An amber light lashes in the helmet faceplate when there are fifteen minutes of normal operation left, and a red light flashes ‘when there are five minutes left. During the course of the adventure you may want these old suits to malfunction for the entertainment of the PCs. Some suggestions * The heating/cooling system, set normaly at default for human body temperature, suddenly kicks on at full power.
